text.title: Gcp Compute Gke Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-compute-gke methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Cluster ops backlog blocks releases; node upgrades skipped.
antipattern.root-cause: Team without dedicated k8s ops capacity runs Standard mode.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to Autopilot OR staff a dedicated SRE for the cluster.

antipattern.symptom: Pods carry node-level SA keys; one compromised pod owns broad GCP IAM.
antipattern.root-cause: GKE cluster created with Workload Identity disabled.
antipattern.fix: Enable Workload Identity; bind k8s SA to Google SA per workload.

antipattern.symptom: Nodes have public IPs; attack surface unnecessarily wide.
antipattern.fix: Migrate to private nodes; set master authorised networks.

antipattern.symptom: Batch jobs and prod workloads contend for the same nodes.
antipattern.fix: Split node-pools by workload class (prod / batch / spot).
